properties.remark: TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED THUNDERSTORM WITH HAIL SIZES UP TO ONE INCH IN DIAMETER.
3 TO 5 INCHES OF WATER ON SEVERAL AREA ROADS.
SPOTTER NEAR WHITEHORSE RANCH REPORTED GOLF BALL SIZED HAIL.
BAKER COUNTY DISPATCH REPORTED FLASH FLOODING THROUGHOUT HUNTINGTON, OREGON. FOUR HOMES HAVE BEEN IMPACTED AND AUTHORITIES ARE ASSISTING WITH SANDBAGGING AND CLEANUP OPERATIONS.
HOT SPRINGS ROAD WAS FLOODED DUE TO HEAVY RAIN.LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED THAT DEBRIS AND SEDIMENT WERE REMOVED FROM THE ROAD AFTER THE WATER SUBSIDED.
WATER ACROSS MANY ROADS ALONG HIGHWAY 78. HIGHWAY 78 CLOSED BETWEEN GRAND VIEW AND WALTERS FERRY...HIGHWAY 45.
MUD SLIDES REPORTED AT LESLIE GULCH SATURDAY 6/13/09.
FLOODING AND DEBRIS ON ROADS WAS REPORTED NEAR AND ALONG STATE ROUTE 78 AT GIVENS HOT SPRINGS AND MILE MARKERS 9 AND 15.
SECTIONS OF WHITE HORSE RANCH ROAD WERE WASHED OUT DUE TO FLASH FLOODING DURING THE MID TO LATE AFTERNOON.
FLASH FLOODING OF A CREEK WEST OF WEISER. WATER OVER OLDS FERRY ROAD. ROAD CLOSED BY COUNTY OFFICIALS.
DIRT ROAD PARTIALLY WASHED OUT - PIERCE PARK BETWEEN CARTWRIGHT RD AND HILL RD. MINOR DAMAGE TO A DIRT SECTION OF NORTH 8TH STREET AS WELL.
WEAK TORNADO OBSERVED BY LAW ENFORCEMENT FORM AND MOVE EAST NORTHEAST ACROSS HIGHWAY 51 4 MILES NORTH OF THE NEVADA BORDER. DUST AND SAGE BRUSH PICKED UP BY TORNADO. ON THE GROUND FOR ABOUT 1 MINUTE.

THUNDERSTORM CAUSED FLASH FLOODING WITH WATER RUNNING ACROSS ROADS. NORMALLY DRY CANALS ARE FULL, AND STANDING WATER UP TO A FOOT DEEP WAS OBSERVED AT BASE OF HILL. 1.3 INCHES OF RAIN FELL IN ABOUT 50 MINUTES.
TRAINED SPOTTER IN SE BOISE /COLUMBIA VILLAGE/ REPORTED THUNDERSTORM WIND DAMAGE - 6 YR OLD ELM TREE SNAPPED IN HALF AND TWO SECTIONS OF FENCE DOWN.
SHINGLES TORN OFF ROOF...TV ANTENNA BLOWN DOWN. VISIBILITY DOWN TO 1/4 MILE IN BLOWING DUST.
50-75 FOOT TALL SPRUCE TREE UPROOTED...DIAMETER 2FEET. POWER OUTAGE.
SPOTTER REPORTS TWO TREES DOWN DUE TO 35-40 MPH WINDS.
SPOTTER REPORTS LARGE CHUNKS OF HAIL A LITTLE SMALLER THAN GOLF BALLS.
